args:
Use the distill skill to extract a complete Allium specification from the
Python code in this directory.

Read every file under ./app and produce one self-contained .allium spec
that describes the observable behaviour of the system. Cover entities,
status enums, state-machine transitions, temporal rules, derived
properties, third-party integrations and webhook entry points.

Follow the distill skill's procedure end-to-end, including any reference
files it instructs you to load and any validation steps it requires
(e.g. running `allium check` and self-correcting). You have access to
Read, Write, Bash and other tools — use them as the skill directs.

Write the final spec to the absolute path: /Users/yavorpanayotov/IdeaProjects/allium-4.0/eval/results/2026-05-16T19-43-09-600Z/experimental/sample-4/spec.allium

Requirements for the saved file:
  - First line must be `-- allium: 3`.
  - No markdown fences or wrappers.
  - Only the spec itself — no preamble inside the file.

After the file is written, you may print a one-line status to stdout.
Only the saved file's content is read by the eval; stdout is for forensics.
